title: thread_local! macro syntax in spec: Ferrum has no macro_rules system
description: ferrum-lang-ownership.md §2.4 shows: 'thread_local! { static COUNTER: Cell<i32> = Cell::new(0); }'. This is Rust macro invocation syntax (foo! { ... }). Ferrum explicitly excludes proc macros and macro_rules. If thread-local storage is a Ferrum feature, it must be expressed in Ferrum syntax (an attribute, a keyword, a library type, etc.). Additionally, Cell::new(0) uses Rust's :: path separator; Ferrum uses . for paths. This code block is pure Rust and should not appear in a Ferrum spec. Fix: either show thread-local storage in actual Ferrum syntax or remove the example.
